figures.caption: Figure 5: Overview of the SentryFuse framework. I. During training (left), the multimodal backbone integrates SentryAttend, replacing dense self-attention with sparse grouped-query attention for an average 13% FLOP reduction. Simultaneously, SentryGate runs in observer mode, learning modality-conditioned structural importance under a curriculum of increasingmodality dropout, guided by a saliency alignment loss. The backbone is optimized via the task loss Lcls, while gate parameters receive gradients only from the alignment objective. II. At inference (right), SentryGate is queried once offline to produce a subnetwork given available modalities and a target compute budget, enabling a train-once, deploy-many paradigm that adapts to (i) varying compute constraints and (ii) arbitrary sensor availability across heterogeneous edge devices.
Table 3: DSADS accuracy under modality dropout and pruning ratios of 6%, 12%, 17%, and 23%. Orange marks backbone-wise local best; bold orange marks global best.
Table 1: WESAD accuracy under modality dropout and pruning ratios of 6%, 12%, 17%, and 23%. Orange marks backbone-wise local best; bold orange marks global best.
Table 2: DaliaHAR accuracy under modality dropout and pruning ratios of 6%, 12%, 17%, and 23%. Orange marks backbone-wise local best; bold orange marks global best.
